channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: Intel rallies after forecast signals a comeback Is underway
item.description: Intel Corp. shares jumped after the chipmaker returned to profitability and gave an upbeat revenue forecast, suggesting that it's making progress on a long and challenging comeback attempt.
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg, The Mercury News

item.title: Why Nvidia just invested in a battery recycling company
item.description: As Nvidia’s value has soared—becoming the first public company to hit $4 trillion in market capitalization earlier this year—it’s been pouring money into AI startups. Its venture arm, NVentures, is also backing less expected bets.
item.dc:creator: Adele Peters, Fast Company

item.title: CISA warns Motex Landscope Endpoint Manager has a worrying security flaw, so patch now
item.description: The critical severity bug is being actively exploited in the wild, possibly by ransomware actors, CISA says.
item.dc:creator: Sead Fadilpašić, TechRadar

item.title: Can’t wait for the touchscreen MacBook Pro M6? This wild, affordable accessory brings touch to your current Mac
item.description: The LapTouch can add touchscreen functionality to any MacBook for a fraction of the cost of a new Mac.
item.dc:creator: Alex Blake, TechRadar

item.title: In a California farming region, researchers are mapping rural heat to protect farmworkers
item.description: In some of the most agriculturally rich regions in the U.S., researchers from San Diego State University are working to understand how climate change is impacting heat in rural areas and the farmworkers who toil in them.
item.dc:creator: Dorany Pineda And Jae C. Hong, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Microsoft has allegedly tasked Xbox to deliver a 30% profit margin, and if true, it could explain the reason behind its price hikes and mass layoffs
item.description: Xbox's job cuts and price increases could be due to a push from Microsoft to achieve a 30% profit margin.
item.dc:creator: Rhys Wood, TechRadar

item.title: Qué medidas pueden tomar los CIO para progresar en la era de la IA
item.description: Si le pregunta a cualquier director de TI sobre la IA, probablemente le dirá que es ideal para realizar tareas repetitivas y de bajo nivel.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: The day the cloud went dark
item.description: This week, the impossible happened—again. Amazon Web Services, the backbone of the digital economy and the world’s largest cloud provider, suffered a large-scale outage. If you work in IT or depend on cloud services, you didn’t need a news alert to know something was wrong.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Musk's rollercoaster year: From boycotts and plunging earnings to a potential trillion-dollar payday
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — If someone left a government job with a black eye, literally, ran a company with shrinking profits, and suddenly had federal investigators crawling over their business, you might say they’re having a bad year. But most people are not Elon Musk.
item.dc:creator: BERNARD CONDON, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Reddit sues Perplexity, three other firms, for AI scraping
item.description: Reddit this week filed suit against Perplexity and three other companies — Oxylabs UAB, AWM Proxy, and Serp Api — for allegedly engaging in so-called AI scraping without authorization.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: How AI can produce detailed storm surge forecasts faster and save lives
item.description: Hurricanes are America’s most destructive natural hazards, causing more deaths and property damage than any other type of disaster. Since 1980, these powerful tropical storms have done more than US$1.5 trillion in damage and killed more than 7,000 people. The No.
item.dc:creator: The Conversation, Fast Company

item.title: PyTorch team unveils framework for programming clusters
item.description: The PyTorch team at Meta, stewards of the PyTorch open source machine learning framework, has unveiled Monarch, a distributed programming framework intended to bring the simplicity of PyTorch to entire clusters.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: This is the world’s first vertical take-off AI-piloted fighter jet
item.description: San Diego-based Shield AI is developing a first of its kind fighter jet: a 2,000-mile-range pilotless plane that takes off and lands vertically and uses artificial intelligence to fly itself, even when adversaries jam navigation and communication systems.
item.dc:creator: Alex Pasternack, Fast Company

item.title: Kevin O’Leary says ‘Marty Supreme’ blew millions by not using AI actors
item.description: The “Shark Tank” star said the production costs are outdated when AI can create realistic looking background characters.
item.dc:creator: Teddy Grant, NewsNation

item.title: Meghan Markle, Prince Harry join call for AI 'superintelligence' ban
item.description: The couple is among more than 700 public figures to sign on to a brief statement by the Future of Life Institute.
item.dc:creator: Judy Kurtz, NewsNation

item.title: Ohio lawmaker proposes comprehensive ban on marrying AI systems and granting legal personhood
item.description: House Bill 469 would prevent AI systems from owning property, serving as executives, or gaining legal personhood in Ohio under Representative Thaddeus Claggett's proposal.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Google and Apple face extra UK scrutiny over 'strategic' role in mobile platforms
item.description: LONDON (AP) — Britain’s antitrust watchdog on Wednesday targeted Google and Apple for their “strategic” roles in mobile ecosystems, opening the door for regulators to impose changes to their business practices to improve competition.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: AI’s dark side shows in Gartner’s top predictions for IT orgs
item.description: While AI certainly has the potential to change enterprise operations for the better, it also can present a few “insidious” challenges for IT leaders, according to analysts at the Gartner IT Symposium/Xpo going on this week in Orlando, Fla.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: AI PCs
item.description: Virtually every aspect of technology has been affected, and potentially improved, by artificial intelligence (AI). PCs are no exception. While the meaning of “AI PC” is still evolving, it generally refers to a computer specifically designed to excel at AI-powered tasks.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: How to use Python dataclasses
item.description: Everything in Python is an object, or so the saying goes. If you want to create your own custom objects, with their own properties and methods, you use Python’s class object to do it.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: DCIM explained: How data center information management powers AI, capacity planning, and sustainability
item.description: Data center infrastructure management (DCIM) involves managing all aspects of the physical data center. DCIM converges IT management, operations management, facilities management, and automation.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: F5 hack highlights persistent supply chain security concerns
item.description: The F5 breach targets a "foundational technology" relied upon by government agencies and critical infrastructure operators. The post F5 hack highlights persistent supply chain security concerns first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Justin Doubleday, Federal News Network

item.title: Netflix blames tax dispute in Brazil for rare quarterly earnings letdown
item.description: Netflix missed the earnings target set by stock market analysts during the video streamer’s latest quarter, a letdown that the company blamed on a tax dispute in Brazil. The results announced Tuesday broke Netflix's six-quarter streak of posting a profit that eclipsed analysts' projections.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL LIEDTKE, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: AI jobs that pay $200K or more
item.description: Instead of fearing what comes next with artificial intelligence, think outside the box. Here are high-earning AI jobs that don't require a computer science degree.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Accelerate together
item.description: Is Agentic AI Your New Cyber Wingman? The post Accelerate together — Secure by AI first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: wfedstaff, Federal News Network

item.title: Florida AG issues subpoena to Roblox for child protection policies
item.description: Attorney General James Uthmeier issued a subpoena to a popular online gaming platform, Roblox, on Monday, demanding information about how the platform sets age-verification requirements, moderates chat rooms, and markets itself to children.
item.dc:creator: Alaina Papazian, NewsNation

item.title: Big Tech is paying millions to train teachers on AI, in a push to bring chatbots into classrooms
item.description: SAN ANTONIO (AP) — On a scorching hot Saturday in San Antonio, dozens of teachers traded a day off for a glimpse of the future. The topic of the day’s workshop: enhancing instruction with artificial intelligence.
item.dc:creator: JOCELYN GECKER, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Amazon cloud outage resolved after downing dozens of major websites, apps
item.description: Amazon Web Services provides infrastructure for many big-name companies, as well as state and local governments.
item.dc:creator: Anna Kutz, NewsNation

item.title: China accuses US of cyberattack on national time center
item.description: BEIJING (AP) — China on Sunday accused the U.S. National Security Agency of carrying out cyberattacks on its national time center, saying any damage to related facilities could have disrupted network communications, financial systems and power supply.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Safety app Citizen to pay users to go live on the platform
item.description: The company posted a social media update, alerting users that they could now be paid for live-streaming incidents that occur around them.
item.dc:creator: Matthew Euzarraga, NewsNation

item.title: AI flaw leaked Gmail data before OpenAI patch
item.description: Cybersecurity experts warn about a ShadowLeak vulnerability that weaponized ChatGPT's Deep Research agent to steal personal data from Gmail accounts through hidden commands.
item.dc:creator: Fox News
